title: When millions of AI agents meet
summary: A discussion with Nenad Tomašev of Google DeepMind on autonomous AI agents that can execute multi-step plans and interact with one another. The conversation covers an "agentic economy" where millions of agents negotiate and delegate, alongside security risks including automation bias, dynamic cloaking, and agentic traps in distributed systems.
writeup: Nenad Tomašev of Google DeepMind outlines an "agentic economy" in which millions of autonomous AI agents transact, negotiate, and delegate tasks to one another. The discussion identifies the operational and safety problems such a system raises: automation bias, cybersecurity threats including dynamic cloaking and agentic traps, and cognitive monoculture across distributed intelligence. The framing matters because coordination at this scale shifts the failure modes from individual model behavior to interactions among many agents.
